title: Soft-Body Flamingo Puppeteer
description: A CesiumMan character stands on a platform controlling a flamingo marionette made of soft-body segments connected by spring constraints. The user clicks and drags control points on the flamingo's wings, neck, and tail to deform its soft body, then triggers the CesiumMan to walk and pull the marionette strings, causing physics-driven ripple deformations across the flamingo mesh.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls loaded from CDN. Load flamingo.glb and CesiumMan.glb. Scene setup: dark gradient background, a wooden platform at y=0 with a subtle grid texture. Place CesiumMan on the left side of the platform at scale 1.5, playing its walk animation on loop. Position the flamingo to the right and slightly above, scaled to 2x. Lighting: one warm directional light from upper-left, one cool fill light from lower-right, ambient light at 0.3 intensity. Implement a soft-body simulation for the flamingo: subdivide its geometry into a mass-spring lattice of at least 40 vertices. Each vertex acts as a particle with mass, connected to neighbors by damped springs (stiffness 0.4, damping 0.02). Apply gravity at -9.8 on the y-axis. Render visible thin lines from CesiumMan's hands upward to 4 control points on the flamingo (left wing tip, right wing tip, head, tail). Interactions required (7 steps with validation): (1) User clicks a control point sphere on the flamingo — it highlights yellow, validate selection. (2) User drags it to deform the soft body — validate displacement exceeds 0.5 units. (3) Release to watch spring relaxation animate back — validate velocity dampens below threshold within 3 seconds. (4) Click a UI button labeled 'Pull Strings' — CesiumMan raises arms, pulling all 4 control points upward by 1.5 units, validate all points moved. (5) Click 'Release' button — points drop under gravity, soft body jiggles, validate oscillation count >= 2. (6) Use a slider (range 0.1–1.0) to adjust spring stiffness in real time — validate the parameter updates the simulation. (7) Click 'Wind Gust' button to apply a lateral force vector to all particles for 0.5 seconds — validate flamingo mesh displaces laterally > 1 unit. Use requestAnimationFrame for the simulation loop at 60fps. Display a small HUD showing current stiffness, particle count, and interaction step progress. OrbitControls should allow camera rotation around the scene.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  flamingoVertexCount: number,         // total vertices in soft-body lattice
  selectedControlPoint: number,         // index of currently selected control point (-1 if none)
  controlPointDragDistance: number,         // cumulative drag distance in world units
  springRelaxationEnergy: number,         // total kinetic energy in spring system
  cesiumManWalkCycles: number,         // count of completed walk animation loops
  marionetteStringTension: number,         // average tension across all control strings
  softBodyDeformationMagnitude: number,         // max vertex displacement from rest position
  userInteractionCount: number,         // total clicks on control points
  isControlPointHighlighted: boolean,         // whether any control point is currently highlighted
  simulationStableFrames: number,         // consecutive frames with energy below threshold
}
